# Set up GitHub scanning

Vault Radar allows you to connect to any organizations on GitHub Cloud and GitHub Enterprise Server.

There are two ways to set up scanning for GitHub -

- Import a single GitHub organization as a data source.

- Import many GitHub organizations together using a data source host.

To import a single GitHub organization as a data source, click **Settings**, then click **Data Sources**.
![Import single GitHub organization as a data source](/img/docs/vault-radar/add-data-source.png)

To import many GitHub organizations together as a data source host, click **Settings**, then click **Data Source Hosts**.
![Import many GitHub organizations as a data source](/img/docs/vault-radar/add-data-source-host.png)

Additionally, for either option, you can choose to scan with [**HCP Vault Radar Scan**](#scan-with-hcp-vault-radar-scan) or
with the [**Vault Radar Agent Scan**](#scan-with-vault-radar-agent-scan).

# Connect to GitHub

For either the data source or data source host option, scanning can be set up using **HCP Vault Radar Scan** or using **Vault Radar Agent Scan**.

## Prerequisites

- Personal Access Token (with the right set of scopes)

![GitHub personal access token scopes](/img/docs/vault-radar/github/github-pat-scopes.png)

## Scan with HCP Vault Radar Scan

1. Select **HCP Vault Radar Scan**, then select either **GitHub Cloud** or **GitHub Enterprise Server**.

1. Depending on your previous selections, **Enter your GitHub domain name** or **Enter your GitHub organization** text field may appear.

![GitHub Enterprise Server scanned by HCP Vault Radar Scan using data source workflow](/img/docs/vault-radar/github/github-enterprise-server-hcp-data-source.png)

1. If you previously selected **GitHub Enterprise Server**, enter the domain URL in the **Enter your GitHub domain name** text field.
    - This will be the domain name of your instance, i.e. `github.acme.com`

1. If you previously selected **Data Sources**, enter the organization in the **Enter your GitHub organization** text field.
    - When navigating to the organization GitHub, the name of the organization should show up

    - For GitHub Cloud, this should be the organization in the URL `https://github.com/ORGANIZATION`

    ![GitHub Cloud organization](/img/docs/vault-radar/github/github-cloud-organization.png)

    - For GitHub Enterprise Server, this should be the organization in the URL `https://github.acme.com/ORGANIZATION`

    ![GitHub Enterprise Server organization](/img/docs/vault-radar/github/github-enterprise-server-organization.png)

1. Enter the personal access token in the **Enter your GitHub personal access token** text field.

1. Click **Next**.

## Scan with Vault Radar Agent Scan

1. Make sure that the agent pool has been set up, refer to the [agent pool documentation](/hcp/docs/vault-radar/agent/deploy) for more information.

1. Select **Vault Radar Agent Scan**, then select either **GitHub Cloud** or **GitHub Enterprise Server**.

1. Depending on your previous selections, **Enter your GitHub domain name** or **Enter your GitHub organization** text field may appear.

![GitHub Enterprise Server scanned by Vault Radar Agent Scan using data source workflow](/img/docs/vault-radar/github/github-enterprise-server-agent-data-source.png)

1. If you previously selected **GitHub Enterprise Server**, enter the domain URL in the **Enter your GitHub domain name** text field.

    - This will be the domain name of your instance, i.e. `github.acme.com`

1. If you previously selected **Data Sources**, enter the organization in the **Enter your GitHub organization** text field.

    - When navigating to the organization GitHub, the name of the organization should show up

    - For GitHub Cloud, this should be the organization in the URL `https://github.com/ORGANIZATION`

    ![GitHub Cloud organization](/img/docs/vault-radar/github/github-cloud-organization.png)

    - For GitHub Enterprise Server, this should be the organization in the URL `https://github.acme.com/ORGANIZATION`

    ![GitHub Enterprise Server organization](/img/docs/vault-radar/github/github-enterprise-server-organization.png)

1. Create an environment variable for the API token in the agent environment.

   **Example:**

   <CodeBlockConfig hideClipboard>

   ```shell-session
   $ export GITHUB_TOKEN=mytoken
   ```

   </CodeBlockConfig>

1. Enter the name of the environment variable in the **Enter environment path to your GitHub personal access token** text field with the `env://` prefix.

    **Example:**

    ```plaintext
    env://GITHUB_TOKEN
    ```

1. Click **Next**.

# Finish importing the data source

1. Choose which repositories to monitor by selecting either the **All active repositories** or **Select repositories to monitor**

    Option 1 ![Add GitHub all active](/img/docs/vault-radar/monitor-all-active-resources.png)

    Option 2 ![Add GitHub custom select](/img/docs/vault-radar/monitor-custom-select-resources.png)

1. Click **Finish**

<Note>

Vault Radar has a limit of 5000 repositories per connection. If you have more
than 5000 repositories, Vault Radar will select the 5000 repositories with the
most recent activity.

</Note>

# Finish importing the data source host

1. Choose which data sources to monitor by selecting either the **All data sources** or **Select data sources**

    Option 1 ![Add GitHub all](/img/docs/vault-radar/monitor-all-active-data-sources.png)

    Option 2 ![Add GitHub custom select](/img/docs/vault-radar/monitor-custom-select-data-sources.png)

1. Click **Finish**

# Update data source

1. Navigate to **Settings**, and then **Data Sources**, and click the three dots to the right

![Update monitored resources](/img/docs/vault-radar/github/github-update-data-source.png)

1. To update the monitored repositories, click **Edit monitored resources**.

1. If you imported the **Data Source** directly, to update the token, click **Edit data source details**.

1. If these data sources were discovered by importing the **Data Source Host**, to update the token, [update the data source host instead](#update-data-source-host)

# Update data source host

1. Navigate to **Settings**, and then **Data Source Hosts**, and click the three dots to the right

![Update monitored data sources](/img/docs/vault-radar/github/github-update-data-source-host.png)

1. To update the monitored data sources, click **Edit data sources**

1. To update the token, click **Edit data source host details**
